constraints.text: The context router cannot depend on embeddings, a vector database, or a reranker.
Context misses cannot trigger default raw-session replay.

decisions.text: The context router performs bounded graph traversal rather than embeddings, vector retrieval, reranking, or broad fallback search.
Routing output is a deterministic context packet with budget, selected artifacts, and raw-session policy.
New information becomes pending updates and retrieval misses become context gaps instead of silent raw-session replay.

requirements.text: The router must export a context-index and sample packets so the dashboard can inspect real graph-governed routing behavior.
Fast, normal, and deep budgets keep raw sessions disabled by default; only forensic mode can read them explicitly.

summary: The context router uses budgeted graph traversal instead of RAG. It builds a context-index, emits route packets, records pending updates and context gaps, and keeps raw sessions forensic-only unless explicitly requested.
